title: Cisco ISE Backup Job Success and Operational-Data Backup Validation

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 9.4.5 (sub-clause around media backup) requires backup-media handling controls. Backup-success monitoring evidences the operational integrity of backups for audit-relevant systems.
Art.21(2)(c) requires backup management. Backup-success monitoring is the primary control.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: UC-17.1.47 alert log + monthly backup attestation.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Art.12 (Backup policies and recovery procedures) — direct alignment.
A1.2 — backup is a primary availability input.
164.308(a)(7) Contingency Plan — backup is a primary requirement.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Misconfigure backup repository credentials. Run a manual backup. ISE emits 51032 (failed). UC-17.1.47 fires showing the (backup_type, repository) with failures > 0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Fix credentials. Successful backup emits 51031. Alert clears on next 4h schedule.

dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Administrative and Operational Audit* category — backup codes `51030` (backup operation started), `51031` (backup completed successfully), `51032` (backup failed), `51033` (backup partial — some data could not be backed up), `51034` (backup repository validation failed), `51035` (operational-data backup completed). Fields: `backup_type` (config|operational|standalone), `backup_repository`, `backup_size_mb`, `triggered_by` (admin|scheduled). ISE OpenAPI `/api/v1/backup/status` polled every 1h, written to `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:backup_status`.
spl:
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (51030, 51031, 51032, 51033, 51034, 51035))
  OR (index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:backup_status")
| eval status = case(MessageCode=="51031" OR MessageCode=="51035", "success", MessageCode IN ("51032","51033","51034"), "failure", MessageCode=="51030", "started", true(), "snapshot")
| stats count latest(_time) as last_run, count(eval(status=="success")) as successes count(eval(status=="failure")) as failures latest(backup_size_mb) as size_mb by backup_type, backup_repository
| eval days_since_success = round((now() - last_run) / 86400, 1)
| where failures > 0 OR (status=="snapshot" AND days_since_success > 2) OR successes==0
description: Detects ISE backup-job failures, missed scheduled backups (>48h without a successful backup), and degraded backups (partial / repository validation failures). Covers config backup AND operational-data backup as separate streams.
value: ISE recovery requires a recent backup. Without a recent valid backup, restoration after PAN failure rebuilds the deployment from the last available, possibly weeks-old, backup — losing all policy work since. Auditing backup health is the primary control for ISE disaster-recovery readiness. Operational-data backup is required for forensic continuity of MnT events; missing this backup means audit data is gone if the MnT node fails.
implementation: Confirm *Administrative and Operational Audit* category at INFO. Deploy OpenAPI poller every 1h. Schedule the search every 4h, search window `-12h@h to @h`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `backup_type` for 12h.
visualization: (1) Single value: days since last successful backup per type. (2) Calendar view: 90-day backup success/failure grid. (3) Top-N: most-failed repositories.

references.title: Cisco ISE 3.4 Admin Guide — Backup and Restore

knownFalsePositives: (a) **First-backup-after-deployment** — Initial backup may take significantly longer than steady-state; suppress for 24h after first scheduled run. (b) **Repository network outage** — A NAS/SFTP outage causes 51034 (repository validation failed); cross-reference network maintenance. (c) **Partial backup during low-disk** — When MnT disk fills, operational-data backup falls back to partial. Cross-ref UC-17.1.29.
mitreAttack: N/A (platform lifecycle — ISE backup status monitoring, no adversary technique)
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Backup repository configured (Administration > Maintenance > Repository).
• Scheduled backup configured (Administration > Maintenance > Backup & Restore > Schedule Backup).
• `backup_repos.csv` lookup with `repository`, `criticality`.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable *Administrative and Operational Audit* category at INFO.

Step 2 — Create the search
Search in `spl` field. Schedule every 4h.

Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as above. Confirm fire.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — Backup Health":
• Days-since-success per backup_type tile.
• 90-day calendar view.
• Repository top-N failures.

Monthly attestation: ISE platform team signs off on backup success report.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Repository auth failure** — Re-store credential.
• **Backup partial — operational data missing** — MnT disk full, cross-ref UC-17.1.29.
• **Schedule never fires** — Schedule disabled in UI.
grandmaExplanation: We make sure ISE's safety copy (the backup) actually completes every day. If the backup fails or skips a day, we ring the bell — without a recent backup, we couldn't restore ISE if something went wrong.
